2604.09841.title: Is There Knowledge Left to Extract? Evidence of Fragility in Medically Fine-Tuned Vision-Language Models
2604.09841.paper_summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) are increasingly adapted through domain-specific fine-tuning, yet it remains unclear whether this improves reasoning beyond superficial visual cues, particularly in high-stakes domains like medicine. We evaluate four paired open-source VLMs (LLaVA vs. LLaVA-Med; Gemma vs. MedGemma) across four medical imaging tasks of increasing difficulty: brain tumor, pneumonia, skin cancer, and histopathology classification. We find that performance degrades toward near-random levels as task difficulty increases, indicating limited clinical reasoning. Medical fine-tuning provides no consistent advantage, and models are highly sensitive to prompt formulation, with minor changes causing large swings in accuracy and refusal rates. To test whether closed-form VQA suppresses latent knowledge, we introduce a description-based pipeline where models generate image descriptions that a text-only model (GPT-5.1) uses for diagnosis. This recovers a limited additional signal but remains bounded by task difficulty. Analysis of vision encoder embeddings further shows that failures stem from both weak visual representations and downstream reasoning. Overall, medical VLM performance is fragile, prompt-dependent, and not reliably improved by domain-specific fine-tuning.
2604.09841.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言模型（VLMs）越來越多地通過特定領域的微調進行調整，但尚不清楚這是否能改善推理能力，超越表面的視覺線索，特別是在像醫學這樣的高風險領域。我們評估了四對開源VLM（LLaVA與LLaVA-Med；Gemma與MedGemma）在四個逐漸增加難度的醫學影像任務中的表現：腦腫瘤、肺炎、皮膚癌和組織病理學分類。我們發現，隨著任務難度的增加，表現會降至接近隨機的水平，這表明臨床推理能力有限。醫學微調並未提供一致的優勢，模型對提示的表述高度敏感，輕微的變化會導致準確率和拒絕率的大幅波動。為了測試封閉形式的VQA是否抑制潛在知識，我們引入了一個基於描述的流程，模型生成的圖像描述供一個僅文本模型（GPT-5.1）用於診斷。這恢復了一定的額外信號，但仍然受到任務難度的限制。對視覺編碼器嵌入的分析進一步顯示，失敗源於弱視覺表徵和下游推理的問題。總體而言，醫學VLM的表現脆弱，依賴於提示，並且不會因特定領域的微調而可靠地改善。
2604.09841.author: Oliver McLaughlin et.al.
2604.09841.authors: Oliver McLaughlin, Daniel Shubin, Carsten Eickhoff, Ritambhara Singh, William Rudman, Michal Golovanevsky
